recommendation: Maintain current settings and economizer operation while outdoor enthalpy/humidity remain favorable. Optionally increase OA fraction to further reduce compressor load if humidity and pressurization constraints allow; verify damper tracking and temperature sensor calibration during routine maintenance.

Maintain current operation; no corrective action needed. If outdoor air humidity/enthalpy and indoor pressurization allow, consider increasing economizer OA fraction to further cut mechanical cooling (potential reduction on the order of ~18 kW_th if driven toward full OA under similar conditions). Continue trending SAT stability and fan kW/cfm to verify sustained performance.

Enable and test economizer cooling now: command OA damper to maximum and verify full stroke and feedback; sequence to use OA up to the SAT limit, then add mechanical. Review economizer enable/lockouts (enthalpy/humidity, low SAT/coil freeze, occupancy/schedule) and relax if overly conservative. Calibrate OAT/RAT/MAT and verify OA airflow measurement. Inspect damper/linkage/actuator and confirm adequate building relief to avoid over-pressurization at high OA. Trend SAT, MAT, OAT, OA fraction, and mechanical cooling for 1–2 days; target OA fraction well above 0.29 during cool outdoor periods while maintaining the 55°F SAT.
